title: penstemon humilis var. humilis
intro: Penstemon humilis var. humilis is a variety of the Penstemon humilis plant, a perennial herb that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and is also known as the "Rocky Mountain beardtongue." Penstemon humilis var. humilis has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
